---
title: CISA SCuBA M365 Secure Baseline (Target) — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: scuba
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: active
phase: phase-1
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-04-15'
aspirational: true
---

# CISA SCuBA M365 Secure Baseline (Target) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `active` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-1`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The CISA SCuBA Baseline Adapter is the **change-making half** of the canonical SCuBA assess-then-remediate cycle. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it applies the declarative CISA SCuBA security posture to M365 tenants — turning the baseline from a published document into the actual configuration the tenant runs.

Unlike its conformance counterpart [`scubagear`](../scubagear/scubagear.html) (which reads tenant state and scores it against the baseline), this adapter is the **writer**: it takes the SCuBA Secure Configuration Baselines as the declarative source of truth and reconciles tenant configuration toward them across seven workloads. The two adapters are designed as a pair — one assesses, one applies — and together they close the loop that makes M365 SCuBA compliance a continuous operational posture rather than a point-in-time audit.

Two primary functions:

1. **Baseline parsing and reconciliation.** Reads the seven-workload SCuBA baseline definitions (AAD, Defender, Exchange Online, PowerBI, Power Platform, SharePoint, Teams) and computes the per-setting reconciliation actions needed to bring the tenant into alignment. The reconciliation report names every setting, the current value, the SCuBA-canonical target value, and the action required.
2. **Baseline application.** Writes the reconciled configuration into the tenant via the workload-specific PowerShell modules and Microsoft Graph. Each write is provenance-stamped and produces a `baseline-apply-manifest.json` entry; subsequent [`scubagear`](../scubagear/scubagear.html) assessments verify that the write took effect and remains in place.

Both halves operate strictly inside the GCC-Moderate tenant boundary, with no commercial-cloud dependencies beyond the documented exceptions in `substrate-manifest.yaml`.

**Implementation:** `src/uiao/adapters/scubagear_adapter.py`. Conformance: 30/30 PASS.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `aad-baseline`, `defender-baseline`, `exo-baseline`, `powerbi-baseline`, `powerplatform-baseline`, `sharepoint-baseline`, `teams-baseline`

**Reads:** CISA SCuBA baseline definitions (seven workloads), tenant configuration via Microsoft Graph and the workload-specific PowerShell modules, and the prior [`scubagear`](../scubagear/scubagear.html) assessment if available (to skip settings already known to be aligned).

**Emits:** `ClaimSet` with baseline-application claims (one per setting written), `EvidenceObject` with provenance per write, and the `baseline-apply-manifest.json` artifact that records every change applied during the run.

**Does NOT:** assess current state (that is [`scubagear`](../scubagear/scubagear.html)'s role), modify configurations outside the declared SCuBA baseline scope, bypass the GCC-Moderate tenant boundary, or apply changes the operator has not approved through the change-control workflow. Apply paths are gated on the `workflow_dispatch` / `repository_dispatch` trigger surface with explicit operator authorization.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CM-2`, `CM-6`, `CM-7`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CM-2** Baseline Configuration | Primary | Applies the declarative SCuBA security baseline to tenant configuration — the canonical mechanism by which the baseline becomes operational rather than aspirational. |
| **CM-6** Configuration Settings | Primary | Writes specific configuration settings per SCuBA workload baseline; every write is provenance-stamped and traceable back to the baseline definition. |
| **CM-7** Least Functionality | Supporting | Baseline application disables unnecessary services and features per SCuBA guidance (e.g., legacy authentication protocols, default-allow sharing settings). |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `powershell-7.4` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Modernization adapter — applies the declarative CISA SCuBA security posture (change-making) to M365 tenants. Distinct from [`scubagear`](../scubagear/scubagear.html) (the read-only conformance assessor) in `canon/adapter-registry.yaml`. Classified per resolved ODA-15 (UIAO_003 §4.7).

Pair with the conformance adapter [`scubagear`](../scubagear/scubagear.html) (`canon/adapter-registry.yaml`), which assesses the applied baseline without mutating it. Together they form the canonical SCuBA assess-then-remediate cycle.

ZTMM pillars: identity, applications-and-workloads, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-002
- UIAO-CANON-003
- https://github.com/cisagov/ScubaGoggles

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
